questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "Have you ever been to New York? ___"
questions.answer: Yes, I have. I went there two years ago.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "Did you see the match last night? ___"
questions.answer: No, I didn't. I went to bed early.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "Has she tried Indian food before? ___"
questions.answer: Yes, she has. She had it at a restaurant last month.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "Did they enjoy the trip? ___"
questions.answer: Yes, they did. They loved every minute of it.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "Have your parents met your new teacher? ___"
questions.answer: Yes, they have. They met him at the school event.

questions.prompt: I _____ (visit) Rome last summer. It was amazing.
questions.answer: visited

questions.prompt: She _____ (never eat) sushi. She wants to try it one day.
questions.answer: has never eaten

questions.prompt: We _____ (see) that film last Friday and we loved it.
questions.answer: saw

questions.prompt: _____ you ever been to a music festival? I went to one last year!
questions.answer: Have

questions.prompt: He _____ (play) really well in the match yesterday.
questions.answer: played

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "I have gone to the cinema last Saturday with my friends."
questions.answer: 'Last Saturday' is a specific past time, so use simple past: 'I went to the cinema last Saturday.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "She called me yesterday and she has told me the news."
questions.answer: Both actions happened at a specific past time (yesterday), so use simple past throughout: 'she told me the news.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "Did you ever visit Japan? I think you would love it there."
questions.answer: 'Ever' with a life experience uses present perfect: 'Have you ever visited Japan?'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "We have watched the match last night and it was incredible."
questions.answer: 'Last night' is a specific past time, so use simple past: 'We watched the match last night.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "My brother studied hard last week and he has passed all his exams."
questions.answer: 'Last week' tells us when it happened, so use simple past throughout: 'he passed all his exams.'
She has never ridden a horse.
Did you enjoy your holiday last summer?
We have visited that museum before.
He won the competition last year.
Have you ever seen a live concert?
